data.narrative:
Castelion, a Series B defense and security company founded by Bryon Hargis, Sean Pitt, and Andrew Kreitz, has raised $100M to date against a current post-money valuation exceeding $1.0B. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 10 companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—within the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, anchoring to the $1.0B last post-money and applying a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $1.9B. The peer-median approach references 6 same-stage, same-sector private companies, including Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), Defense Unicorns ($1.0B), and Heven AeroTech ($1.0B), yielding a median of $1.1B across the cohort, with a range of $300M to $3.0B.

These methods triangulate to a median valuation of $1.5B, with a low of $956M and a high of $2.2B. The $956M floor reflects conservatism against peer-median positioning; the $2.2B ceiling incorporates stage-progression momentum. Primary valuation uncertainty stems from the wide dispersion in peer multiples—a 10x gap between minimum and maximum comparable—reflecting heterogeneous risk profiles and growth trajectories within early-stage defense tech.
